frameworks.name: shared topology of spatial vision
frameworks.one_line: crowding, localization, and saccades indexed by a common position-signal precision map

frameworks.name: retinal/cortical resource heterogeneity
frameworks.one_line: covariation argued to exceed what nonuniform sampling alone explains

frameworks.name: position signal precision
frameworks.one_line: extends a unified precision mechanism from crowding/saccades to spatial localization

core_question: Do crowding, spatial localization, and saccadic precision share a common underlying mechanism across the visual field?
key_assumptions: visual sensitivity varies systematically with eccentricity and upper-lower visual field
covariation across tasks indicates a shared mechanism rather than independent processes
adaptation can probe whether tasks tap the same population of position signals
manipulations: visual field location (foveal vs peripheral; upper vs lower)
key_findings: all three measures decline with eccentricity and show upper-lower asymmetry
across subjects, worst crowding co-occurred with worst localization and least precise saccades
motion adaptation reduced precision in all three abilities similarly
interpretations: crowding, localization, and saccadic precision share a common mechanism
this mechanism reflects the precision of position signals throughout the visual system
